idea.hook: Think you've truly left the Solar System?
facts: Our Sun's gravitational influence (the theoretical edge of the Oort Cloud) extends up to 100,000 AU (about 1.5 light-years) away.
Voyager 1, our fastest spacecraft, travels at ~17 km/s. At this speed, it would take Voyager ~26,000 years to reach this far.
Even if we could achieve 1% of light speed (nearly 3,000 km/s), a journey to this distant boundary would still take ~150 years.
idea.payoff: Truly breaking free from our own star's cosmic grasp is a multi-generational odyssey, even with advanced propulsion!
idea.title: Escape the Sun's Grasp? ☀️🚀

scenes.text: Think you've left the Solar System?
scenes.visual: A dark, starry expanse. A faint, distant glow suggests the Sun, getting smaller as if receding.

scenes.text: The Sun's gravity reaches 100,000 AU (1.5 light-years) to the Oort Cloud!
scenes.visual: A small, bright Sun icon at the center. A vast, transparent sphere expands outwards, labeled '100,000 AU' and 'Oort Cloud' at its edge.

scenes.text: Voyager 1 (~17 km/s) would take 26,000 YEARS to reach it.
scenes.visual: A stylized Voyager 1 spacecraft icon slowly drifts from left to right across a deep space background, leaving a long, faint trail.

scenes.text: Even at 1% light speed (3,000 km/s), it's a 150-year journey!
scenes.visual: A sleek, futuristic spacecraft streaks quickly across the screen, leaving a bright, long-streaked path. Stars blur in the background to emphasize speed.

scenes.text: Breaking free from our Sun's grasp is a multi-generational odyssey!
scenes.visual: A breathtaking, wide shot of the Milky Way galaxy, vast and swirling. Our tiny Solar System (a single star and faint orbits) is a minuscule dot, dwarfed by the galaxy's immensity.
